_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: TX state-legislature batch (Phil King SD-10, Joanne Shofner HD-11, Mayes Middleton SD-11->AG, Tan Parker SD-12, Trey Wharton HD-12). Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence. Roster/currency: King's SD-10 is UP in 2026 (prior record's 'not up' label is stale); Middleton is a sitting SD-11 senator who WON the 2026-05-26 GOP runoff for TX Attorney General (dual-office framing); Parker's SD-12 is NOT up until 2028 (record's 2026 next-election flag is the default-incumbent error). Shofner's prior 100/100 profile cited mismatched sources (a TN rifle PAC, wrong member page) - rebuilt from primary ratings. Wharton's prior file had contradictory metadata - rebuilt.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Phil King (R) holds TX Senate District 10 (took office Jan 2023; former TX House HD-61 1999-2023; former Fort Worth police officer). SD-10 is a 4-yr seat won in 2022, so it IS up in Nov 2026 and he is running as incumbent - the prior record's 'Not up at next scheduled election' label is stale and should be corrected. No named 2026 opponent confirmed. Chairs the Senate Select Committee on Religious Liberty and Homeland & Border Security. Scored on his real roll-call/authorship record + issue-group ratings.

phil-king@TX.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025, 89th Lege). HOUSE AUTHOR of HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry); SENATE AUTHOR of SB10 (2025 Ten Commandments in classrooms, signed) + SB1515 (2023) + religious-speech package; sponsor of SB12 (2025, bans DEI/SOGI considerations and LGBTQ clubs in public schools, signed); voted with the prevailing 19-R Senate majority on SB2 (2025 ESA/school choice). Wall of law-enforcement endorsements (CLEAT, TMPA, Fort Worth POA) + only former police officer in the Legislature; NRA-PVF + TSRA PAC endorsed. Chairs Homeland & Border Security and backed Operation Lone Star. No documented contrary (anti-conservative) vote located - no FALSE scored.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025, 89th Lege); Texas Right to Life endorsed; sponsored SB1207 (2025) adding adoption education - consistent affirmative pro-life record.

1.note: No abortion-abolition (vs restriction) sponsorship located; not inferred.

2.note: No position located on embryonic stem-cell/IVF embryo discard.

3.note: No position located on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: Sponsor of SB12 (2025), which bars DEI/SOGI considerations and LGBTQ clubs in public schools (signed) - opposes LGBTQ promotion in public-school policy.

0.note: Voted with the prevailing 19-R Senate majority on SB2 (2025 ESA/school choice; only R defector was Nichols); endorsed by Texas Home School Coalition - supports school choice + homeschool freedom.

2.note: SB12 (2025, his sponsorship) bars SOGI/DEI considerations in public K-12 schools.

0.note: Senate author of the 2025 religious-liberty package incl. SB965 protecting public-school employee religious speech/prayer; chairs the Senate Select Committee on Religious Liberty.

3.note: Senate author of SB10 (2025 Ten Commandments in public-school classrooms, signed) and predecessor SB1515 (2023) - supports public-square Christian symbols/prayer.

0.note: Only former police officer (15 yrs Fort Worth PD) in the Legislature; endorsed by CLEAT, Texas Municipal Police Assn, Fort Worth POA - affirmative pro-law-enforcement record.
Chairs the Senate Select Committee on Homeland & Border Security; backed Operation Lone Star funding (DPS/National Guard border presence).
House AUTHOR of HB1927 (2021 Texas constitutional/permitless carry, signed); endorsed by NRA-PVF and Texas State Rifle Association PAC.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Joanne Shofner (R) is the sitting TX House Rep for District 11 (Nacogdoches/East Texas; member ID 4755), seated Jan 2025 after unseating GOP incumbent Travis Clardy in the 2024 primary; running for reelection in 2026. NOTE: the prior 100/100 profile cited mismatched/invalid sources (a Tennessee rifle PAC; the wrong member page) and is rebuilt here from primary issue-group ratings. Only her pro-life rating is issue-specific HARD evidence; she has one short session and her per-cell votes are largely undocumented in available sources, so most cells are null (not inferred). Her SB2/voucher vote is reported inconsistently across secondary sources and was NOT scored pending the official House Journal roll call.

joanne-shofner@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Profile rebuilt - prior 100/100 rested on mismatched citations. HARD: Texas Alliance for Life 90% (2025, 89th Lege); ranked in YCT's top-5 most-conservative legislators (89th). Endorsed 2024 by Abbott, Trump, Cruz, Perry. The score rests primarily on the documented pro-life pillar; her per-issue voting record is otherwise thin in public sources and intentionally left null rather than inferred. SB2 (ESA/voucher) vote unresolved - secondary sources conflict; do not score until the capitol.texas.gov roll call is pulled (possible R-defection worth a follow-up check).

2.note: No position located on embryonic stem-cell/IVF.

set.office: Texas State Senator, District 11 (sitting · 2026 R nominee for Texas Attorney General)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Mayes Middleton (R) currently holds TX Senate District 11 (Galveston/Brazoria; took office Jan 2023; founded the Texas Freedom Caucus as a TX House member 2019-2023). He is NOT seeking SD-11 reelection - he WON the 2026 GOP nomination for TEXAS ATTORNEY GENERAL, defeating Chip Roy 55.2%-44.8% in the 2026-05-26 runoff (open seat, Paxton departing), and faces Democrat Sen. Nathan Johnson in the November general while remaining the sitting SD-11 senator. Scored on his real Senate/House roll-call + authorship record. FOLLOW-UP: check for a separate AG-candidate record to de-duplicate against this sitting-seat record.

Freedom Caucus founder behind HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry); YCT career ~93. No documented contrary vote located - no FALSE scored.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Tan Parker (R), TX Senate District 12 (Denton/Tarrant), Senate Majority Leader; TX House 2007-2023 then Senate from 2023. CURRENCY FIX: he won SD-12 in Nov 2024 for a 4-yr term ending Jan 2029, so he is NOT up in 2026 - next regular election is 2028 (the record's '2026 / primary' next-election flag is the default-incumbent error and should be corrected to 2028). Scored on his real roll-call/authorship record + issue-group ratings. Note: a 72% JBS Freedom Index reflects an establishment-leadership profile, but on the specific rubric cells below his record aligns and no documented contrary vote was found.

tan-parker@TX.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. CURRENCY FIX: SD-12 not up until 2028 (was flagged 2026). Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025); Texas Values Action 100% (2025, 'Faith & Family Champion'). AUTHORED SB1558 (2023 specie legal tender); voted YES HB1056 (2025); supported SB2 (2025 school choice), SB11 (prayer in schools), SB965 (Coach-Kennedy religious-speech law), HB229 ('What is a Woman Act'), SB1257 (Detransitioners Bill of Rights). AUTHORED SB36 (2025 DPS Homeland Security Division/border); voted YES HB1927 (2021 carry) + SB1362 (2025 Anti-Red-Flag Act); Vice Chair Senate Criminal Justice + backed the 2025 bail overhaul. Deviation notes (NOT rubric-contrary): voted NO on HB2 (school-finance appropriation, a fiscal vote) and NO on HJR98 (Article V convention) - neither maps to a documented anti-conservative rubric position, so left unscored, not FALSE.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Trey Wharton (R) is the sitting TX House Rep for District 12 (East Texas/Brazos Valley - Walker/Huntsville, Grimes, Madison, Robertson, Washington; member ID 4795), seated Jan 2025; ran unopposed in the 2026 GOP primary and faces a Democrat in November.
